description: Phase 3 tasks — close every Phase 1/2 gap that left the UI half-built, ship the Connectors OAuth UX, promote entity_refs to canonical entities, build Accounts as the second first-party app using only the public SDK, wire cross-app capability + event flow, and finish every placeholder screen in the shell. The phase exits as a fully manually-testable product. Sequential execution per way-to-implement.md.
phaseGate: A fresh tenant signs up, lands on Home, opens Connectors → connects real Gmail via OAuth → emails sync into Inbox → generic-triage classifies + generic-replier drafts in parallel → user installs CRM via Apps → CRM email-lens enriches the SAME inbox item without re-classifying → user installs Accounts (built only with the public SDK) and is prompted to grant entities.crm:read → user closes a deal in CRM → Accounts auto-drafts an invoice linked to that deal via entity_refs → copilot answers questions across BOTH apps simultaneously → uninstalling CRM warns about Accounts dependency. All shell placeholder screens (Copilot, Approvals, Drive, Activity, Team) render real data. Manual test plan in docs/tests/test_phase3.md walks every step end-to-end.
phaseRisk: If Accounts requires SDK changes, the SDK contract is wrong — pause and fix the SDK before opening third-party in Phase 4. If canonical entities require breaking changes to CRM, document the migration; the rule is no SDK breakage during the canonical promotion.

tasks.name: Connectors page replaces placeholder
tasks.goal: Real Connectors page listing every registered connector kind, with per-tenant connected-account rows, status badges, and an Add button per kind.
acceptance: Renders all kinds from /api/admin/connectors/registry plus connected accounts from /api/admin/connectors
Per-card status: not connected | connected | error | expired (with last-error tooltip)
Add button is enabled per kind based on what's been registered with the framework
Vitest + RTL covering empty / connected / mixed states
doNot: Hardcode Google or Slack — render the registry generically
Inline OAuth logic; that lands in N2/N3
tasks.notes: Connectors page replaces placeholder. Pure presenter (connectorsPresenter.ts) handles status normalization + view-model + sorting; ConnectorCard.tsx renders a single card; index.tsx wires the page; connectorsApi.ts wraps the existing /api/connectors/status + /api/connectors/disconnect/:kind endpoints. 17 unit tests on the presenter cover the full state matrix. Backend already had /api/connectors/oauth/:kind/{authorize,callback} from Phase 1 — those get refined in N2/N3 if needed.

tasks.name: OAuth authorize redirect endpoint
tasks.goal: GET /api/connectors/:kind/authorize uses the existing OAuthManager to generate a state-signed URL and 302s to the provider's authorize endpoint.
acceptance: 302 with the provider's authorize URL when called by an authenticated session
State token includes tenantId + nonce; signed with auth.secret to detect tampering
Returns 404 for unregistered kinds; 401 unauthenticated; 400 if connector kind has no oauth config
doNot: Re-implement OAuth — use connector SDK's existing OAuthManager
Generate a new client secret per request — tenant-level config lives in env or settings
tasks.notes: Authorize endpoint refined: HMAC-SHA256 signed state via @boringos/connector/oauth-state.ts (createState/verifyState/isSafeReturnTo). returnTo allowlist rejects open redirectors. Connector clientId is now properly typed via OAuthCredentialed; missing clientId surfaces a 500 with a clear message instead of trying with empty creds. shellOrigin config knob added to BoringOSConfig for cross-origin returnTo. 12 unit tests on the state helpers.

tasks.name: OAuth callback + credential persist
tasks.goal: GET /api/connectors/:kind/callback exchanges code for tokens, encrypts + writes a connectors row for the tenant, and redirects back to the shell's /connectors page with success/error querystring.
acceptance: Verifies state signature; rejects on mismatch with a 400
On success: upserts a connectors row with kind, name (provider account label), encrypted credentials, scopes, status='connected'
Redirects to /connectors?connect=success&kind=:kind on success, ?connect=error&reason=... on failure
Integration test against Google's test OAuth endpoint via mock; verifies idempotent re-connect updates the same row
doNot: Trust the redirect URI from the querystring — must match what was signed in N2
Store unencrypted refresh tokens
tasks.notes: Callback flow rewritten alongside N2 in the same connector-routes.ts file. State signature verified before tenant resolution. Tokens upserted (active status). Success → redirect to returnTo with ?connect=success&kind=. Failure → redirect with ?connect=error&kind=&reason= so the shell page can surface the error to the user. Plain-text error pages eliminated.

tasks.name: Add Google / Add Slack wizard modal
tasks.goal: Click Add on a connector card → modal explains the OAuth scopes that will be granted → click Authorize → window.location.href to the authorize endpoint.
acceptance: Modal title + description sourced from the connector manifest, not hardcoded per kind
Scopes rendered as a human-readable list with a 'why we need this' tooltip
Authorize button kicks the user to /api/connectors/:kind/authorize
Cancel closes without writing anything
doNot: Build separate modals per connector — single generic modal driven by manifest data
Hide scopes — the user has to see what they're granting
tasks.notes: AddConnectorModal.tsx — generic modal driven entirely by manifest data (name, description, scopes). humanizeScope() helper renders Google URL scopes as gmail.modify/calendar.events; Slack-style passes through. Esc closes; click-outside cancels. Reconnect bypasses the modal since the user has already granted these scopes. /api/connectors/status now returns oauthScopes in the response. 3 new presenter tests covering scope humanization.

tasks.name: Default sync workflow installs on connect
tasks.goal: When a Google connector is created, install the Gmail sync workflow template (cron 15m → list_emails → routeToInbox) so the user immediately starts seeing emails in Inbox without further action.
acceptance: After successful OAuth callback, a per-tenant workflow row + cron trigger exist
Disconnecting the Google connector pauses (not deletes) the workflow
Re-connecting resumes the workflow at the same cron
Test: end-to-end — call /authorize, mock Google callback, assert workflow exists and is scheduled
doNot: Hardcode the Gmail sync workflow into the framework — the connector defines its own default-workflow set
Skip the connector if the user already had one — the row is per-tenant, idempotent on reconnect
tasks.notes: Added DefaultWorkflowSpec interface + ConnectorDefinition.defaultWorkflows() extension point in @boringos/connector. Google connector exports the Gmail sync spec (cron */15 → list_emails → for-each → create-inbox-item). New @boringos/core/connectors/post-connect.ts owns install + pause + resume; idempotent via spec.tag stored in workflow name prefix. Wired into OAuth callback + disconnect handler. 5 integration tests against embedded Postgres.

tasks.name: Disconnect + token-expired UX
tasks.goal: Per-card Disconnect button revokes credentials at the provider, deletes the row, pauses dependent workflows. Token-expired status surfaces with a Reconnect CTA that re-runs the OAuth flow.
acceptance: Disconnect: confirmation modal, revoke at provider, delete row, pause workflows; surfaces success or partial failure
Token refresh failure flips connector status to 'expired'; UI shows a yellow banner
Reconnect runs the same authorize flow but with hint=:tenantId so providers reuse the existing account if possible
Test: simulate token revoked at provider; next workflow run flips status; UI updates
doNot: Drop the workflow data on disconnect — the user may reconnect; preserve unless the user explicitly removes
Block disconnect if a workflow is running — let it complete then pause
tasks.notes: DisconnectModal.tsx — confirmation step with explicit consequences (tokens removed, workflows paused, data preserved). Token-expired badge already in N1 presenter; Reconnect button shows in that state and skips the Add modal. /connectors?connect=success|error&kind=&reason= surfaces inline banners with dismissible UX; URL cleaned via history.replaceState so refresh doesn't redisplay.

tasks.name: Connector status surface in shell chrome
tasks.goal: Header indicator (small bell or status dot) shows aggregate connector health; click → flyout listing per-connector status; routes to /connectors on click of a degraded item.
acceptance: Healthy state hidden / muted; degraded state visible with a count badge
Polls or subscribes to /api/events for connector.* events
Click → routes to /connectors with the degraded card scrolled into view
doNot: Spam the bell on transient errors — debounce; show only after second failure
Build a full notifications panel here — that lands in S7
tasks.notes: ConnectorsHealthIndicator.tsx — compact pill in shell main area's top-right. Polls /api/connectors/status every 60s. Hidden on happy path; surfaces only when expired or error states exist. Click expands a flyout listing degraded connectors with a 'Manage connectors →' deep link. Mounted in Layout.tsx between Sidebar and Outlet.

tasks.name: Schema for canonical_persons + canonical_companies
tasks.goal: First-class tables for canonical Person + Company. Include identity fields (email, phone, name) + dedup-friendly indexes (lowercased email, normalized phone).
acceptance: canonical_persons: id, tenantId, displayName, primaryEmail (citext), primaryPhone, createdAt, mergedIntoId (nullable for soft-merges)
canonical_companies: id, tenantId, displayName, primaryDomain, createdAt, mergedIntoId
Unique index on (tenantId, lower(primaryEmail)) — soft, partial WHERE primaryEmail IS NOT NULL
Migrations applied; smoke test inserts + queries
doNot: Force an email or phone — names alone allowed (e.g. lead with just a Slack handle)
Hard-delete on merge — use mergedIntoId so apps can follow the chain

tasks.name: Promote API: app entity → canonical entity
tasks.goal: POST /api/admin/canonical/persons/from-entity { sourceApp, sourceType, sourceId } resolves an app entity into a canonical Person, creating one if no match, otherwise linking via entity_refs.
acceptance: Match heuristic: exact lower(email) → existing canonical Person; otherwise create new
Always writes an entity_refs row linking sourceEntity ↔ canonicalPerson
Idempotent: calling twice with same source returns the same canonicalId
Same shape for /from-entity → canonical Company (domain match instead of email)
doNot: Auto-merge across email + phone in the same pass — surface candidates, the merge requires a separate explicit call
Trust client-supplied canonicalId — always re-resolve

tasks.name: Auto-link heuristic on entity create
tasks.goal: When CRM (or any app) creates a new Contact, the install pipeline's hook layer fires a canonical-resolve so a CRM Contact and a future Accounts Customer with the same email both back-reference the same canonical Person.
readFirst: apps/generic-triage/src/agents/triage.ts (so triage can attach canonical Person to inbox items)
acceptance: All apps that emit entity_created get an automatic canonical resolve via subscription, not by calling a helper
If a duplicate is detected, the entity's metadata gains canonicalCandidates: [...] for UI to surface a merge prompt
Test: simulate CRM Contact + Accounts Customer with same email → entity_refs has 2 rows pointing to same canonical Person
doNot: Block the entity create on resolution — async via event handler
Auto-merge silently — score-based candidates surface to UI; human picks

tasks.name: Cross-reference badge on entity detail
tasks.goal: When viewing a CRM Contact who is also an Accounts Customer, a 'This person' badge lists all app references with deep-links.
acceptance: Reads /api/admin/canonical/persons/:id/refs and renders one chip per app reference
Clicking a chip routes to that app's entity detail page
Hidden when only one app refers to the canonical entity (no point showing self)
doNot: Render anything if canonical_persons isn't enabled for the tenant — feature flag this for safety
Allow editing canonical fields from this badge — that's the merge UI in O5

tasks.name: Merge UI for duplicate canonical entities
tasks.goal: When auto-link detects candidates, surface a 'These look like the same Person — merge?' prompt with field-by-field comparison and Confirm/Reject.
acceptance: POST /api/admin/canonical/persons/:id/merge { intoId } sets sourceId.mergedIntoId, rewires entity_refs
All app entity rows that previously linked to merged-source now resolve to merge-target via canonical resolution
UI confirms via diff view; merge is a single click
Reject flow records a non-match marker so we don't re-prompt
doNot: Hard-delete the source canonical row — soft-merge via mergedIntoId chain
Do reverse-merge automatically — admins explicitly choose target

tasks.name: SDK helpers for canonical lookup from apps
tasks.goal: Public helpers — useCanonicalPerson(personId), getCanonicalForEntity({sourceApp, sourceId}) — exposed from @boringos/app-sdk so future apps don't have to know about the canonical schema.
acceptance: Hook returns the canonical Person + all entity_refs back to source apps
Returns null + sets metadata.canonicalCandidates when ambiguous
Documented in docs/app-sdk.md with a worked example
@boringos/app-sdk minor version bump; no breaking changes (M5 contract)
doNot: Force apps to use canonical entities — opt-in only; CRM keeps its own Contact rows linked via entity_refs
Ship a hook that fetches synchronously — async with TanStack Query like every other shell hook

tasks.name: Manifest declares cross-app entity reads
tasks.goal: App manifest gains capabilities: ['entities.crm:read'] declarations. Validator checks the referenced app exists in the registry at install time.
acceptance: Schema accepts capabilities matching ^entities\.[a-z][a-z0-9-]*:(read|write|admin)$
Validator: install fails if entities.crm:read is declared but CRM isn't installed in the tenant
Existing capabilities like inbox.items:write keep working (no breaking change)
Test: manifest with entities.crm:read installs cleanly when CRM present, errors otherwise
doNot: Add a finer-grained per-entity-type capability yet — single entities.{app}:read covers everything for v1
Allow * wildcards across apps — explicit-only

tasks.name: Permission prompt shows cross-app deps
tasks.goal: Phase 1's permission prompt (already renders capabilities at install) gets a 'this app needs to read entities from CRM, Accounts' section with the affected app names + scope.
acceptance: Cross-app capabilities grouped under a separate visual section with the granting app's logo
Approve button disabled if any cross-app dep refers to an uninstalled app, with 'Install X first' CTA
Test: permission prompt for Accounts shows the CRM dep block with the right copy
doNot: Allow Approve to silently install the missing dep — explicit user action
Re-render the existing capability list — extend, don't replace

tasks.name: Runtime enforcement on cross-app entity reads
tasks.goal: When Accounts agent reads a CRM Contact via the canonical-resolve helper, a capability check ensures Accounts has entities.crm:read; missing capability → 403 with a clear error.
acceptance: Middleware reads the calling app's tenant_apps row, checks capabilities array, and either passes through or returns 403
Error body: { error: 'capability_denied', missing: 'entities.crm:read', requestedBy: 'accounts' }
Test: stub Accounts without entities.crm:read tries to resolve a CRM Contact — gets 403
doNot: Trust client-claimed app id — derive from the agent's session/tenant_app row
Cache deny decisions — read-through on every call (Drizzle's connection pool handles the cost)

tasks.name: Uninstall cascade warning when other apps depend
tasks.goal: Uninstalling CRM while Accounts has entities.crm:read declared shows a warning listing the dependent apps and what will break, with options: Uninstall both / Just CRM (Accounts will fail) / Cancel.
acceptance: /api/admin/apps/:appId?dry-run=true returns dependent apps; UI surfaces them
Uninstall both option chains uninstalls in dependency order (Accounts first, then CRM)
Just CRM option proceeds with a 'capabilities will fail' acknowledgement and writes an audit row
doNot: Block uninstall — always offer 'Just CRM' as a power-user option
Cascade by default — explicit selection

tasks.name: Scope checkpoint — confirm Accounts scope with user before any Q work
tasks.goal: Pause sequential execution. Re-confirm with the user that thin-AR (auto-draft invoice from deal_won + reconcile payments) is the right scope, vs alternatives (broader Accounts, Support, Scheduling, etc.). Record the decision in this task's notes before unblocking Q1.
readFirst: docs/build/tasks-phase-3.json (Q1-Q8 to see the full surface)
acceptance: User has explicitly confirmed which app to build as the second first-party app
Confirmed scope is recorded in this task's status notes
Q1-Q8 task names + goals updated if the chosen app is not Accounts
doNot: Skip this gate — the cost of building the wrong second app is the cost of the whole phase
Assume Accounts because the JSON says so — the JSON was a starting point

tasks.name: Bootstrap boringos-accounts repo
tasks.goal: New repo at hebbs-clients/boringos-accounts following the same shape as boringos-crm. Created via the create-boringos CLI scaffolder so we exercise that path too.
readFirst: Existing boringos-crm/feat_business_shell branch for the layout reference
acceptance: Repo bootstrapped via npx create-boringos --kind=app boringos-accounts
boringos.json valid against current schema
package.json has @boringos/app-sdk as a workspace dep (alpha)
Empty defineApp() exported with id='accounts'
doNot: Copy CRM code — start clean to test the scaffolder honestly
Skip the scaffolder and hand-create — that bypasses the contract test

tasks.name: Schema: invoices, payments, chart of accounts
tasks.goal: Domain tables for Accounts. fin_invoices, fin_payments, fin_chart_of_accounts. Migrations applied via Phase 2's K2 schema runner.
acceptance: fin_invoices: id, tenantId, customerCanonicalPersonId (FK to canonical_persons), dealCanonicalRef, amountCents, currency, status, dueAt
fin_payments: id, tenantId, invoiceId (FK), amountCents, paidAt, source
fin_chart_of_accounts: id, tenantId, code, name, type
Install runs migrations cleanly; uninstall (hard) drops them
doNot: Use raw cents in display — UI formats with locale; storage uses cents
Cross-tenant FK — every row is tenant-scoped

tasks.name: Agents: Invoice Drafter + Payment Reconciler
tasks.goal: Two agents seed at install. Invoice Drafter watches deal_won → creates a draft invoice. Payment Reconciler watches incoming payment events → matches to invoice.
readFirst: boringos-crm's agent definitions for the pattern
acceptance: Both agents register on install via Phase 2's K3 agent registrar
Skill markdown teaches each agent its scope (no overlap)
Invoice Drafter writes drafts to fin_invoices via the app's own /api/accounts route
Payment Reconciler edits fin_invoices.status = 'paid'
doNot: Use entities.crm:read inside the agents themselves — they read the canonical Person via the SDK helper
Auto-send invoices — drafts sit until human approves

tasks.name: Workflow templates: deal_won → draft_invoice
tasks.goal: Workflow that subscribes to crm.deal_won, resolves the deal's customer to a canonical Person, then wakes Invoice Drafter with the deal context.
acceptance: On crm.deal_won (sourced from CRM): resolve canonical Person, write a fin_invoices draft row, wake Invoice Drafter with the new invoiceId
If the deal has no customer (rare), workflow halts with a clear log line
Idempotent on duplicate deal_won events (e.g. retry) — uses dealCanonicalRef as dedup key
doNot: Subscribe to private CRM events — only documented public ones (deal_won, contact_changed, etc.)
Skip canonical resolution and embed CRM contactId directly — that breaks if CRM uninstalls

tasks.name: UI slots: Invoices, Customers, Settings
tasks.goal: Three slot.pages contributions: Invoices list + detail, Customers list + detail (canonical Person view filtered to those with invoices), Settings panel for chart of accounts.
acceptance: All pages mount via slot system; sidebar grows with Accounts section after install
Invoice detail shows linked deal (deep-link to CRM if installed)
Customer detail uses the CanonicalRefBadge from O4
Settings panel edits chart of accounts entries
doNot: Re-implement the canonical Person view — reuse the shared component
Hardcode tenant settings keys — read from the framework's settings table

tasks.name: EntityActions for fin_invoice
tasks.goal: Actions on an invoice: Mark Paid, Send Reminder, Cancel. Wire into ui.entityActions slot.
acceptance: Actions render on fin_invoice detail toolbar
Mark Paid creates a fin_payments row with source='manual'
Send Reminder enqueues an email via Gmail connector if connected
Cancel updates status='cancelled'; reversible via 'Reactivate' action
doNot: Build action handlers that call CRM APIs directly — go through canonical layer + capability checks
Auto-send reminders without explicit click

tasks.name: Manifest validation + capability declarations
tasks.goal: Final boringos.json declares: entityTypes (fin_invoice, fin_payment), capabilities including entities.crm:read, ui slots, schema migrations, agents, workflows. Validates against the SDK schema.
readFirst: boringos-crm's final boringos.json for the shape
acceptance: validateManifest() passes
verify-connectors-style validator passes (referenced files exist, declared events exist, etc.)
Capabilities array includes inbox.items:write, entities.crm:read, network.send (Gmail emails)
minRuntime matches the framework version
doNot: Declare entities.crm:write — Accounts only reads CRM data; deal updates flow back via events, not direct writes
Skip validation — manifest must pass before install

tasks.name: Install + e2e smoke
tasks.goal: Install Accounts via the Apps screen on a tenant that already has CRM. Verify the cross-app capability prompt fires (P2) and post-install the sidebar grows with Accounts entries.
readFirst: Phase 2's M-suite gate tests for the pattern
acceptance: Install via /api/admin/apps/install with manifest URL pointing at boringos-accounts repo's raw boringos.json
Permission prompt blocks until CRM is verified installed (P2)
Post-install: 3 nav entries appear, 2 agents seed, 1 workflow seeds, capabilities are recorded with the cross-app entry
Test against embedded Postgres; uninstall cleans up
doNot: Skip the permission prompt verification — it is the cross-app contract
Hardcode app id mapping — derive from manifest

tasks.name: CRM emits crm.deal_won when a deal closes
tasks.goal: When a deal moves to a Closed-Won stage in CRM, a crm.deal_won event fires with { dealId, canonicalCustomerId, amountCents, closedAt }.
readFirst: boringos-crm/src/agents/* and any place stage transitions are handled
acceptance: Stage change to a stage flagged stage.outcome='won' emits the event
Event payload schema documented in CRM's manifest events[]
No emission when toggling stages within Closed-Won (idempotent on dedup key dealId)
doNot: Emit from the UI directly — server-side after the DB write commits
Embed CRM-specific shape — payload fields must be canonical-resolvable

tasks.name: Accounts subscribes via workflow
tasks.goal: Accounts' deal-won-draft workflow (Q4) is triggered by the real crm.deal_won event from R1, not a stub.
acceptance: Real CRM deal_won fires Accounts workflow within 5 seconds
Workflow run record visible on Accounts' Workflows page
Test: trigger CRM deal_won via direct DB insert + event emit, observe Accounts workflow run
doNot: Poll for events — subscription only
Couple the workflow to CRM internals — only public event payload

tasks.name: Invoice draft has prefilled deal context
tasks.goal: When R2 creates a fin_invoices draft, the draft contains: customer (resolved canonically), deal description, amount from deal, line item summary.
acceptance: Draft includes deal title, amount, customer name, suggested due date (default: 30 days)
Agent run log shows the workflow context it received
User can edit any field before sending
doNot: Auto-send — draft remains until user approves and clicks Send
Lock fields — every field is editable; agent suggestions are suggestions

tasks.name: Bidirectional entity_refs link
tasks.goal: After invoice draft creation, an entity_refs row links crm_deal:dealId ↔ fin_invoice:invoiceId so both detail pages show each other.
acceptance: CRM deal detail shows 'Linked invoice: INV-001' chip with deep-link
Accounts invoice detail shows 'From deal: ACME-Q1' chip with deep-link
Test: navigate both directions
doNot: Re-derive the link from canonical resolution — entity_refs is the authoritative cross-app link
Block the workflow on link creation — best-effort

tasks.name: Reverse: invoice paid → CRM deal annotation
tasks.goal: When a payment is reconciled (Q3 Reconciler), an event fires that CRM listens for; CRM annotates the linked deal with a paymentReceivedAt timestamp.
acceptance: invoice.paid event payload includes the linked dealCanonicalRef
CRM deal detail surfaces 'Paid on YYYY-MM-DD' in the activity timeline
Reverse direction proves the event flow works both ways
doNot: Mutate CRM deal directly from Accounts — events only
Couple the deal annotation to a single payment — handle partial payments by accumulating

tasks.name: Copilot conversation UI replaces placeholder
tasks.goal: Real conversation interface: message stream, input box, tool-use cards, agent-typing indicator. Backend already exists (/api/copilot/sessions).
acceptance: Sidebar list of recent sessions; click → load conversation
Send message → optimistic add → SSE stream surfaces agent response as it generates
Tool-use cards render inline (e.g., 'Created deal ACME-042') with deep-links
New conversation button creates a fresh session via /api/copilot/sessions
doNot: Reimplement message storage — comments table is the system of record
Custom polling — use the existing SSE stream

tasks.name: Approvals UI replaces placeholder
tasks.goal: Real approvals page: filterable list (pending/approved/rejected), card per item, decision UI (approve/reject + comment).
acceptance: List paginated; filter by status + age
Card shows: requesting agent, what's being approved (with diff/preview), submitted at, urgency
Approve/Reject calls /api/admin/approvals/:id/{approve|reject}
Realtime: approvals stream via SSE so list updates without refresh
doNot: Build a separate detail page — card expands inline
Auto-decide based on rules — that's a future workstream

tasks.name: Drive UI: file browser
tasks.goal: Replace Drive placeholder with a real file browser: folder tree, file list, upload, download, delete, move.
acceptance: Tree built from /api/admin/drive/list
Drag-drop upload to current folder
File row: name, size, lastModified, download + delete
Move via right-click → choose folder
doNot: Re-implement multi-part upload — use what's in the admin API today
Show framework-internal files (drive_skill_revisions etc.) — filter to user-owned

tasks.name: Drive skill editor + revision history
tasks.goal: Per-tenant Drive skill (the markdown teaching agents how to use Drive) gets a markdown editor with revision history (revert / view diff).
acceptance: Editor with monaco or codemirror; preview pane
Save → posts to /api/admin/drive/skill, increments revision
Revisions list: each row has author, timestamp, action menu (revert / diff)
Diff renders as a side-by-side markdown comparison
doNot: Bring in a heavy WYSIWYG — markdown source is the system of record
Auto-save on every keystroke — explicit Save button

tasks.name: Activity log UI replaces placeholder
tasks.goal: Real activity stream: timeline grouped by day, filters (actor, entity type, action type, date range).
acceptance: Timeline: today, yesterday, this week, older
Each row: actor (user or agent name+avatar), action verb, target, timestamp, deep-link
Filters apply server-side via querystring
Pagination via cursor; infinite scroll
doNot: Show framework-internal events (run_started etc.) by default — they go behind a 'system' filter toggle
Allow editing or deleting entries — audit log is immutable

tasks.name: Team management UI replaces placeholder
tasks.goal: Real team page: members list, role per member, invite form, pending invitations list, role change, remove.
acceptance: Member list: name, email, role (admin/member), joined, last active
Invite modal: email + role; submits via POST /api/auth/invite, shows the invite link or sends email
Pending invitations: separate section with revoke button
Admin-only actions hidden from member-role users
doNot: Allow self-removal — admins must transfer ownership before leaving
Build SSO / SCIM — that's Phase 5 enterprise tier

tasks.name: Notifications dropdown
tasks.goal: Bell icon in chrome with badge count; click → flyout listing recent events (run failed, approval needed, connector expired, etc.). Already-read state persisted.
acceptance: Subscribes to /api/events; aggregates the last 50 actionable events
Click on a notification → routes to the relevant screen and marks read
Mark all read button
Keyboard shortcut: Cmd+Shift+N
doNot: Build push notifications — in-app only for v1
Spam every event — only actionable types (run_failed, approval_needed, connector_expired, …)

tasks.name: Inbox detail + actions
tasks.goal: Click into an inbox item → full content view (email body, attachments, thread history); inline actions (mark read/snooze/archive/convert-to-task), agent annotations rendered (triage classification, reply drafts, CRM lens enrichment).
acceptance: Detail: subject, from, full body (rendered safely), attachments list
Annotations panel: triage classification + score + rationale; reply drafts (collapsible); CRM lens (collapsible)
Actions: archive, snooze (1h/4h/tomorrow/custom), mark unread, convert to task
Convert to task creates with the email content prefilled
doNot: Render raw HTML emails — use a sanitizer (e.g. DOMPurify)
Hide annotations from a single app — every annotation visible

tasks.name: Onboarding wizard UI
tasks.goal: First-time tenant sees a 5-step wizard: connect Gmail → install CRM → invite team → review pipeline stages → tour. State stored in onboarding_state (already in DB).
acceptance: Auto-shows after signup if onboarding incomplete; dismissible
Each step deep-links to the actual surface (Connectors / Apps / Team / Settings)
Steps mark complete via the existing complete-step endpoint
Final step = a guided tour pop-overs through the chrome
doNot: Force completion — every step skippable
Build the tour engine in-house — use a small library like react-joyride if needed

tasks.name: End-to-end timing benchmark
tasks.goal: Measure: signup → connect Gmail → install CRM → install Accounts → close deal in CRM → invoice draft visible in Accounts. Phase 3 target: under 90 seconds total (excluding the human OAuth click).
acceptance: Vitest suite simulates the full flow against embedded Postgres + mocked OAuth callback
Per-step timings logged; bottleneck visible if gate fails
doNot: Test against real Google in this benchmark — real Gmail is T2's job
Skip the canonical-resolution step in the timing measurement — it is on the hot path

tasks.name: Real Gmail end-to-end (manual)
tasks.goal: Manual run against a real Google account: connect Gmail via the new Connectors UI, send a test email from another account, verify generic-triage classifies + generic-replier drafts + CRM email-lens enriches the same item, confirm under 60s wall-clock.
acceptance: Test user can complete the flow without instructions outside test_phase3.md
All three annotations visible in inbox detail (S8)
No console errors during the run
Recorded screencap or step-log committed alongside the result
doNot: Skip if Gmail flake — re-run; flakes are bugs
Use a shared test account — tester uses their own Gmail with consent screen 'testing' mode

tasks.name: Cross-app capability prompt verification
tasks.goal: Installing Accounts shows the entities.crm:read prompt with CRM dep visualized. Approve → install. Reject → install aborted. Install when CRM not present → blocked.
acceptance: Test 1: CRM installed → prompt visible with the CRM section → approve → install succeeds
Test 2: CRM not installed → install button blocked with 'Install CRM first' CTA
Test 3: Reject prompt → install aborts cleanly, no partial state
doNot: Skip the no-CRM negative case — it is the security-relevant one
Mock the prompt — test against the real React component

tasks.name: Cross-app uninstall cascade verification
tasks.goal: Uninstalling CRM with Accounts present surfaces the cascade warning (P4). Both options ('uninstall both', 'just CRM') work as documented; 'just CRM' is followed by Accounts agent runs failing with capability_denied (P3).
acceptance: Modal correctly lists Accounts as dependent
Both options behave as P4 specifies
After 'just CRM', Accounts agent run with cross-app read returns 403 capability_denied (verifies P3)
doNot: Force the 'cascade' option — power users may explicitly choose breakage
Hide the audit row for the breakage choice — must be findable in Activity

tasks.name: Copilot answers across both apps
tasks.goal: Copilot prompt 'What deals are open and what invoices are pending?' returns a unified answer using both CRM agentDocs + Accounts agentDocs.
readFirst: Phase 2's M4 (CRM agentDocs in copilot context)
acceptance: Copilot context includes both CRM and Accounts API mount headers
Test prompt's response references both sets of entities
Tool-use cards render correctly for actions across both apps
doNot: Hand-curate the prompt — use the same context-provider pipeline real users hit
Skip the assertion on tool-use cards — they prove the action plumbing

tasks.name: SDK regression check
tasks.goal: Diff @boringos/app-sdk between Phase 2 close and Phase 3 close. Only allowed change is the canonical helpers added in O6 (additive, no breaking).
acceptance: Verifies only additive changes in app-sdk (canonical.ts + index.ts re-export); validate.ts and manifest-schema.ts unchanged
Connector-sdk completely unchanged
Phase 3 SDK contract: PASS or FAIL with explicit reasons
doNot: Pass the gate if the SDK was bent during Accounts build — pause the phase, fix the SDK, redo the broken work
Allow accidental breaking changes hidden in unrelated PRs — the check is decisive

tasks.name: Manual e2e test plan: docs/tests/test_phase3.md
tasks.goal: Comprehensive manual test plan covering signup → Gmail → CRM → Accounts → cross-app event flow → all shell screens. Pre-flight, smoke pass, full pass, known issues, decision gate.
acceptance: Step 0 pre-flight (build, install)
Step 1 unit tests (aggregate count + new files added)
Step 2 connector verifier
Step 3 manual signup + sidebar check
Step 4 Gmail OAuth + first email round-trip (real)
Step 5 CRM install + email-lens check
Step 6 Accounts install + cross-app prompt + capability deny test
Step 7 deal-close → invoice draft (cross-app event flow)
Step 8 every shell screen: Copilot conversation, Approvals, Drive, Activity, Team, Notifications, Inbox detail, Onboarding
Step 9 Copilot multi-app prompt
Step 10 Uninstall cascade (cascade + just-CRM both tested)
Smoke pass (10 min) shortlist; full pass (90 min) ordering
Known pre-existing issues + Phase 4 deferrals
doNot: Reference internal milestones (T1-T7 task IDs) in user-visible test steps — describe the user behavior
Defer the manual checks for Phase 4 — Phase 3 close requires this run
